canto_title: The Approach to the City of Dis and the Encounter with Filippo Argenti

scenes.summary: Dante describes seeing two small flames and a distant signal atop the tower.
Dante asks Virgil about the mysterious lights, and Virgil explains they mark what is expected ahead.
A small boat approaches the travelers, steered by a single ferryman.
Virgil rebukes the ferryman Flegias, who reacts with anger.
Dante and Virgil board the boat, which immediately cuts through the water with unusual speed.

scenes.scene_name: The Encounter with the Muddy Spirit
scenes.summary: A grotesque, mud-covered spirit confronts the travelers in the swamp.
The spirit embraces Dante and delivers a bitter prophecy about the proud kings who will suffer in Hell.
Dante expresses a wish to see the proud spirit fully submerged in the mud.
The travelers witness the violent struggle of Filippo Argenti in the mud.

scenes.scene_name: Arrival at the Walls of Dis
scenes.summary: As they approach the City of Dis, Dante notices the walls glowing red from the internal fires.
The gatekeepers of the city aggressively challenge the travelers and demand they return via the path they came.
Dante is overcome by fear and begs Virgil not to abandon him.
Virgil comforts Dante, promising that their path cannot be taken from them.
Virgil leaves Dante alone at the gate as the inhabitants of the city retreat into chaos.
The gates close, leaving Virgil dejected and mourning his inability to enter.
Virgil tells Dante not to fear, revealing that a secret, unguarded entrance exists nearby.
